question: '\nProve that you can use dominoes to tile a rectangular checkerboard with of squares.\n'
response: It looks like part of the problem statement is missing—specifically the dimensions of the checkerboard.  A very common version of this problem is:  > Prove that a (2^n \times 2^n) checkerboard with one square removed can be tiled using dominoes (or L-shaped trominoes).  Or another classic:  > Prove that an (m \times n) checkerboard can be tiled with dominoes iff at least one of (m, n) is even.  Right now your sentence says:  > “tile a rectangular checkerboard with of squares”  —so the key numbers are missing.  Could you resend the full problem? Once I have the exact statement, I’ll give you a clean proof 👍
